skill.name: firebase-app-hosting-basics
skill.description: Deploy and manage web apps with Firebase App Hosting. Use this skill when deploying Next.js/Angular apps with backends.
skill.summary: Deploy and manage web apps with Firebase App Hosting
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: All 75 static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation and reference materials describing Firebase CLI commands and configuration patterns. No actual code execution, credential access, or network requests occur. The detected patterns are markdown documentation examples, not executable code.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Pattern in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged `npx -y firebase-tools@latest` commands in markdown as Ruby/shell backtick execution. This is a documentation/reference skill that describes CLI commands. The backticks indicate code formatting in markdown, not actual execution. No command injection vulnerability exists.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matches CLI command examples in markdown documentation, not executable code with user-controlled input.

low_findings.title: Network URL Reference in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged https://console.firebase.google.com/project/_/overview URL as hardcoded URL. This is documentation pointing users to Firebase Console for billing plan upgrade. No network request is made by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static URL reference in documentation, not an actual network call from the skill.

low_findings.title: API Key Placeholder in Configuration Examples
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged API_KEY variable names as generic API/secret keys. These are placeholder examples in YAML configuration documentation showing how to configure environment variables. No actual secrets are accessed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Placeholder variable names in configuration examples, not actual secret access.

content.user_title: Deploy web apps with Firebase App Hosting
content.value_statement: Setting up Firebase App Hosting manually requires navigating complex documentation. This skill provides ready-to-use commands and configuration templates for deploying Next.js and Angular applications to Firebase Cloud Run infrastructure.
actual_capabilities: Initialize App Hosting backends with firebase-tools CLI
Configure apphosting.yaml with Cloud Run settings and environment variables
Manage backends including list, get, and delete operations
Set up secrets using Cloud Secret Manager integration
Create GitHub-based CI/CD workflows for automated deployments
Test configurations locally using Firebase Local Emulator Suite
limitations: Only supports Next.js, Angular, and similar SSR frameworks
Does not handle application-specific build configuration beyond standard npm builds

use_cases.title: Deploy a Next.js application
use_cases.description: Set up a complete App Hosting backend with environment variables and secrets for a production Next.js app with SSR.
use_cases.target_user: Web developers migrating from Firebase Hosting to App Hosting

use_cases.title: Configure secrets management
use_cases.description: Store API keys and sensitive credentials securely using Cloud Secret Manager and make them available to App Hosting backends.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers setting up secure credential handling

use_cases.title: Set up automated GitHub deployments
use_cases.description: Connect a GitHub repository to App Hosting for automatic deployments on git push.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams wanting streamlined CI/CD workflows

prompt_templates.title: Initialize App Hosting
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Firebase App Hosting for my Next.js project in the current directory

prompt_templates.title: Configure environment variables
prompt_templates.prompt: Add environment variables for my API keys in the apphosting.yaml file

prompt_templates.title: Deploy to production
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my Angular app using firebase-tools with the apphosting backend

prompt_templates.title: Set up GitHub CI/CD
prompt_templates.prompt: Connect this repository to Firebase App Hosting for automatic deployments on push

output_examples.input: Set up Firebase App Hosting for my Next.js project
output_examples.output: Backend created successfully. Your app will be available at: [region]-[project-id]-[backend-id].a.run.app. Use 'firebase-tools deploy' to trigger deployments.

output_examples.input: Add a secret for my API key
output_examples.output: Secret 'API_KEY' created in Cloud Secret Manager. Grant access to your backend with 'apphosting:secrets:grantaccess API_KEY'.

best_practices: Always use Cloud Secret Manager for sensitive credentials instead of hardcoding values
Set minInstances to 1 or higher for production apps to avoid cold start latency
Configure separate backends for staging and production environments
anti_patterns: Do not store actual API keys in apphosting.yaml values - use secrets instead
Avoid deploying from source for production - use GitHub integration for version control
Do not skip the secrets:grantaccess step when reusing existing secrets

faq.question: What pricing plan do I need for App Hosting?
faq.answer: App Hosting requires the Firebase Blaze (pay-as-you-go) pricing plan. The Spark (free) plan is not supported.

faq.question: Which frameworks does App Hosting support?
faq.answer: App Hosting supports Next.js, Angular with SSR, and other frameworks that use Node.js server-side rendering.

faq.question: How do I test my app locally before deploying?
faq.answer: Use 'firebase-tools emulators:start --only apphosting' to run the App Hosting emulator locally on port 5004.

faq.question: How do I manage secrets securely?
faq.answer: Use 'firebase-tools apphosting:secrets:set' to store secrets in Cloud Secret Manager, then reference them in apphosting.yaml.

faq.question: Can I use environment variables during build time?
faq.answer: Yes, set availability to BUILD for variables needed during npm run build, or RUNTIME for serving requests.

faq.question: How do I roll back a deployment?
faq.answer: Use 'firebase-tools apphosting:rollouts:create --git-commit <hash>' to deploy a specific previous commit.
